hard.next_step: Confidence 50% 22nd June - Confirmed we are shortlisted (top 3), but due to the war, all non-essential projects are paused by exec team, expecting to restart in July/August, with deeper demonstrations, and then commercial negotiations, with a decision likely in Q3. 17th June - Scheduled call moved to Mon 22nd June. 10th June - Champion update call scheduled Weds 17th June. 5th May - Further clarifications received based on our request for more info, will need to analyse and respond asap. 24th April - Clarifications submitted, requested a call to review and discuss next steps. 12th April - RFP clarifications received, (a lot) deadline is Fri 24th April - some updated eProc/Intake Merlin user numbers and another requirement to outline what modules are related to each of the ~500 requirements, will prioritise after SAMI RFP. 30th Mar - RFP submitted within the deadline, still missing some metrics for pricing so made some assumptions and positioned it as a budgetary estimate. 27th Mar - Commercial clarifications received at the 11th hour - now tight for a Monday RFP submission. 23rd Mar - Nudged again for clarification response, will need to build out commercials accurately ahead on 30th RFP Deadline. 13th Mar - Clarification responses delayed, (needed for pricing) RFP extended to 30th March (not our request) - answers should be with us in a few days, will help build out a more accurate commercial response. 18th Feb - RFP received today, not read in detail yet, looks like: iAnalyze, iSupplier, CLM, iSource maybe iRisk & Intake) - deadline of 28/2 will be tight with Tanya's travel, may need an extension - next step to review docs in depth and raise clarifications for response build. 11th Feb - Successful (but short) demo of iSupplier, iRisk & iSource, Next Steps: Tushar wants another session on Spend Analysis (and a Rate Card call off mechanism/work-around) in next week or two (sharing dates ASAP), no decision yet on whether to use our biz case support, will talk to CFO. He has agreed that we can meet/pitch Zycus to CFO (+maybe MD) after the next session. Initial timelines have slipped but working towards a decision by end June. Has future potential to add CLM. 28th Jan - Demo rescheduled for 11th Feb Jan 8th - Caught up with Tushar, he is a little swamped with end of year, will update us for the rescheduled demo sometime over next couple of weeks. Dec 17th - Tushar postponed the scheduled demo for 18th, will reach out to rearrange asap. DEc 11th - Positive Discovery Call with Tushar, leading S2P evaluation, interested in Supplier Mgt, Sourcing and Agentic AI (mostly ANA) - next step live demo scheduled for 18th DEc. Live Demo scheduled for December 18 th -Thursday, 09:30 AM Oman/UAE time
hard.ais_why:
• Per the 12 Apr 2026 rep note from Dan Quinn: 'RFP clarifications received, (a lot) deadline is Fri 24th April - some updated eProc/Intake Merlin user numbers and another requirement to outline what modules are related to each of the ~500 requirements.' The buyer is asking specifically for updated Merlin user numbers, which signals Merlin/Intake is in scope and being sized - but it's commercial sizing, not an AI vision statement.
• Per the 18 Feb 2026 rep note: 'RFP received today... looks like: iAnalyze, iSupplier, CLM, iSource maybe iRisk & Intake) - deadline of 28/2 will be tight.' The RFP product mention is functional (iAnalyze/iSupplier/CLM/Intake), not 'AI Needs / Agentic / autonomous sourcing.' No SAMI/Tushar/buyer quote naming Merlin or Agentic AI as a selection criterion is captured.
• Medium confidence: Products in scope include Merlin (and rep notes mention Intake Merlin user counts), NextStep is dense across ~10 dated entries, but Avoma fuzzy-matched to a different account (SwWDuQA - no recordings) so no buyer transcript. RFP-driven cycle without explicit AI evaluation criterion quoted from a named buyer.

north_star_verdict.headline: Zycus is confirmed one of three shortlisted vendors (22 June 2026) and Tushar Patel remains engaged, but the 1 October close date is not credible: Arabian's exec team has frozen all non-essential projects due to the regional war, with a July/August restart followed by deeper demos, commercials and CFO/MD approval still ahead. The deal is healthy and live, the date will slip into Q4 2026 or beyond.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 2025-12-12 (207 days old). Stage Shortlisted, close 2026-10-01 (87 days out). Time in Shortlisted since 22 June 2026 confirmation; RFP submitted 30 Mar 2026, shortlist confirmed 22 Jun 2026. Remaining steps: restart (Jul/Aug) then deeper demos then commercials then CFO+MD sign-off then contract. That chain cannot complete by 1 Oct 2026; close will slip to Q4 2026+. Last buyer touch: email 17 Jun, Next Step update 22 Jun 2026.
evidence: Buyer confirmed shortlist (top 3) on 22 June 2026 (SF Next_Step__c) - deal is live and progressing.

Exec team paused all non-essential projects due to the regional war; restart expected July/August 2026 with deeper demos then commercial negotiations, decision likely Q3 2026 (SF Next_Step__c 22 June 2026).
Structured RFP process executed: RFP received 17 Feb, submitted 30 Mar, clarifications exchanged Apr/May 2026 (SF activities).
No approved budget; Tushar stated on 11 Dec 2025 the CFO decides in collaboration with the MD and budget is not yet assigned - neither exec engaged 5+ months later.
Single-threaded on Tushar Patel (Procurement Specialist); no CFO/MD relationship (SF contact roles, Avoma).
Close date already pushed 2026-08-28 to 2026-10-01 on 22 Jun 2026; amount trimmed 200k to 175k (field history).
Best Case is indefensible: no engaged economic buyer, no assigned budget, external pause - recommend Pipeline until restart and exec engagement are confirmed.

metrics.narrative: Tushar Patel articulated only qualitative pain: on the 11 Dec 2025 discovery call he said the aim is 'to streamline the processes, build more robust and transparent system' because 'procurement is always a gray area', across a 30-50 person procurement team spread over Muscat, UAE, Saudi and India. Scale is partly sized (~200 users for e-procurement and Merlin against ~2,646 staff, ~$1bn revenue per the April RFP clarifications) but no ROI, savings, cycle-time or headcount figure has ever been quantified. Zycus offered its business-case service twice (11 Dec 2025 and 11 Feb 2026); Tushar deferred both times, saying he would 'talk to the relevant stakeholder move is to build a concise ROI model tying Zycus to the manual-process/opacity pain to anchor the $175k and unlock the unassigned budget.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer role is known but the person is neither named nor engaged. On 11 Dec 2025 Tushar was explicit: 'I have to report my decision to our CFO. CFO will take the decision in collaboration with our MD', and separately confirmed 'budget is not yet assigned, and we are still figuring out'. So the CFO owns budget and the MD co-decides, but five months on neither has been met and Tushar's 11 Feb 2026 commitment to 'arrange one call along with CFO' has not been delivered. No Salesforce contact role exists for the CFO or MD, so they cannot be named from evidence. The move is to convert Tushar's offered CFO introduction into a booked high-level session before the July/August restart.
sources: Avoma demo call 11 Feb 2026 (Tushar: agreed to arrange CFO/MD pitch)
SF OpportunityContactRole (only Tushar Patel on record)

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well documented through the RFP and the 11 Feb 2026 demo. The buyer scored Zycus against ~500 functional requirements mapped to modules (SF Next_Step__c 12 Apr 2026), with confirmed interest in sourcing/negotiation (iSource), supplier management and onboarding with approved-vendor-list management, spend analysis (iAnalyze), and autonomous negotiation (ANA/Merlin); Tushar explicitly flagged low interest in contract lifecycle management and risk on the 11 Feb call. Two hard technical criteria surfaced: bidirectional Epicor ERP integration (Tanya confirmed feasible, 11 Feb) and multi-metadata approved-vendor-list management for client-specific RFQ floating (Tushar, 11 Feb). One exposed criterion: automatic call-off/purchase-order generation from fixed rate-card agreements, which Tanya confirmed Zycus cannot do automatically (only blanket-PO with manual release).
sources: Avoma demo call 11 Feb 2026 (Tushar Patel, Tanya Rajesh)

decision_process.narrative: The process is clearly mapped from the activity sequence: discovery (11 Dec 2025), live demo of iSupplier/iRisk/iSource (11 Feb 2026), formal RFP received (17 Feb), Zycus response submitted (30 Mar), multiple clarification rounds (Apr-May), shortlist to top three (22 Jun 2026). It is now frozen by an exec-imposed pause on non-essential projects due to the regional war, with restart targeted July/August, then deeper/use-case demos, then commercial negotiations, then a CFO-and-MD approval, then contract (SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun 2026). Tushar runs the project himself and reports up to the MD; he prefers a phased, module-by-module rollout over big-bang because of limited change-management appetite (11 Dec 2025).

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet visible because the deal is pre-vendor-selection: no MSA/Order Form/SOW, legal, procurement channel or InfoSec discussion has occurred, and there is no incumbent contract expiration to act as a forcing function (this is greenfield digitization replacing manual processes plus Epicor and an in-house vendor portal). As an Oman/APAC new-business S2P deal it will ultimately need a full-suite MSA plus Order Form plus SOW and is likely PO-gated regionally. Visibility will only come once Zycus is selected; the way to create it early is to agree a mutual close plan with Tushar naming procurement, legal and InfoSec owners at restart.
sources: Avoma discovery call 11 Dec 2025 (current stack: Epicor + in-house vendor portal, manual contract management)

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is clearly articulated but unquantified. Arabian Industries manages contracts manually and runs procurement on Epicor plus an in-house vendor portal; Tushar described procurement as 'always a gray area' and the goal as building 'more transparency, more efficiency so that we can get more productivity' across a 30-50 person team in four countries (11 Dec 2025). The pain owner is Tushar Patel (procurement/ERP transformation lead). It is confirmed qualitative pain - transparency, control and efficiency in a manual, multi-region operation - with no cost, error-rate or cycle-time figure attached, which is the single gap the ROI work must close.

champion.narrative: Tushar Patel (Procurement Specialist per the contact role; signs emails as ERP Analyst) is the sole engaged champion. He initiated discovery (11 Dec 2025), ran the RFP end-to-end (Feb-Mar 2026), handled every clarification round, and reconfirmed Zycus in the top three on 22 Jun 2026 - genuine driving activity. What is still missing keeps him 'developing' not 'strong': he is mid-level, his access to the MD is unverified, he has not delivered the MD introduction he offered on 11 Feb 2026 (five months elapsed), and there is no evidence he is actively advocating Zycus over GEP, Ariba or Ivalua. He is at risk if the executives deprioritize S2P on restart.

competition.narrative: Zycus is confirmed one of three shortlisted vendors (22 Jun 2026). The named field carries Coupa, GEP Worldwide, Ivalua, JAGGAER and SAP Ariba, and the 11 Feb 2026 MEDDPICC note reads 'assume Ariba, Ivalua, GEP ... maybe not Coupa due to scope'. No buyer has ever expressed a preference for any rival, and there is no S2P incumbent to unseat - the real alternative is the manual status quo plus Epicor. The wedge is Zycus's Merlin agentic negotiation agent (ANA), demoed on 11 Feb and of clear interest to Tushar, plus confirmed bidirectional Epicor integration and a phased rollout that avoids ERP replacement. The genuine risk over the multi-month pause is that a peer secures CFO-level business-case engagement first.
sources: Avoma demo call 11 Feb 2026 (ANA/Merlin, Epicor integration)

deal_movement.summary: The deal advanced from Formal Evaluation to a confirmed top-three shortlist on 22 Jun 2026, then was frozen by an exec-imposed pause on non-essential projects due to the regional war. In the same window the close date was pushed to 1 Oct, the amount trimmed to $175k, forecast raised to Best Case, and the account reassigned from Dan Quinn to RVP Mohamad Alhakim. Last genuine buyer touch: email 17 Jun and Next Step update 22 Jun 2026.

items.change: Stage moved Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted; buyer confirmed Zycus in the top three
Exec team paused all non-essential projects (regional war); restart expected July/August 2026
Forecast category raised Pipeline to Best Case
Account reassigned from Dan Quinn to Regional VP Mohamad Alhakim
Amount trimmed 176,400 to 175,000 (earlier cut 200,000 to 176,400 on 31 Mar)

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is confirmed shortlisted (top 3) as of 22 Jun 2026 with no rival stated as preferred and no S2P incumbent to displace (greenfield replacing manual processes + Epicor). Carried from the last read: GEP Worldwide is the strongest current peer threat (best-fit S2P peer in the region), SAP Ariba and Ivalua are credible enterprise-suite threats, Coupa has faded on scope/fit, and JAGGAER appears only in the SF field with no buyer mention. No new competitor call evidence surfaced this run, so rankings are held as carried. The larger current threat is not a vendor but inertia: budget is unassigned and the project is paused, so a rival winning CFO-level business-case engagement during the freeze is the real risk. Wedge: Merlin agentic negotiation (ANA) plus confirmed Epicor integration and phased rollout.

competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the agile AI-native alternative with faster Epicor integration and phased rollout versus Ariba's heavyweight footprint.

competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate on agentic AI (not just analytics) and the seamless Epicor add-on model; confirm Ivalua cannot match it.

competitors.how_we_win: Coupa likely faded on scope/fit; confirm with Tushar post-restart whether it is out of contention.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.how_we_win: No buyer mention in any call; validate status with Tushar at restart rather than assume it is live.

The one exposed criterion is automatic purchase-order generation from fixed rate-card agreements, which Zycus supports only via manual blanket-PO release.

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers dominate at this shortlisted, exec-paused, single-threaded stage.
flags: Multi-thread to power now: the CFO owns budget and the MD co-decides, yet both remain unmet five months after Tushar offered a CFO introduction.
Anchor the phased $175k with a quantified ROI case; the buyer has stated only qualitative transparency/efficiency pain and has not assigned budget.
Lead the deeper demos with Merlin agentic negotiation (ANA) to differentiate versus GEP, Ariba and Ivalua while no rival is yet preferred.

items.name: Tushar Patel
items.title: Procurement Specialist
items.risk: Mid-level; access to CFO/MD unverified; has not delivered the CFO introduction offered 11 Feb 2026; could be overruled if execs deprioritize S2P
items.sentiment: Positive: driving the evaluation, confirmed top-3 shortlist and the July/August restart

champion_strength.summary: Tushar Patel is the sole engaged champion and has genuinely driven the deal (discovery, full RFP, top-3 shortlist), but he is mid-level, his access to the CFO/MD is unproven, and he has not delivered the exec introduction he offered on 11 Feb 2026. Developing, not strong, and at risk if executives deprioritize S2P at restart.
champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole; Avoma calls 11 Dec 2025 and 11 Feb 2026; SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin is in scope and the autonomous negotiation agent (ANA) was demoed and drew interest on 11 Feb 2026, but the buyer is evaluating on functional S2P fit rather than an AI vision, and the agentic story has not been put in front of the CFO/MD. Under-positioned relative to its differentiating potential versus GEP/Ariba.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 5.0 (Moderate) is fair: Tushar is receptive ('we are always welcoming AI... planning to introduce AI in some operational area', 11 Dec 2025) and Merlin user numbers are being sized on the RFP, but AI is a nice-to-have, not the stated selection criterion. The calls corroborate the field rather than raising it.

items.detail: Economic buyer (CFO) and decision maker (MD) never engaged; deal single-threaded on a mid-level specialist
No budget assigned; the $175k has not been validated by the CFO
Exec-imposed war pause with July/August restart makes the 1 Oct close date not credible
Scope reduced to ~$175k and a deliberately phased rollout with limited big-bang appetite - a defensive cost/change signal
items.source: Avoma 11 Dec 2025; field history amount cut

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is current but thin: buyer reconfirmed on 22 Jun and answered emails through mid-June, yet no buyer call has been recorded since the 11 Feb demo and the project is externally frozen. Not cooling by choice - paused by exec mandate.
items: Last transcribed buyer call: 11 Feb 2026 (nearly five months ago)
Recent touches are email (17 Jun) and Next Step update (22 Jun) - rep-chased, buyer-confirmed
External pause, not disengagement: buyer reaffirmed intent to restart July/August

scope_change.from: ~$200k full-suite S2P intended across all regions
scope_change.to: $175k phased rollout (~200 e-proc/Merlin users) starting with priority modules
scope_change.detail: Amount cut from $200k (Mar) to $175k (Jun) and the buyer explicitly wants a phased, module-by-module rollout given limited change-management appetite; CLM and risk were deprioritized on the 11 Feb demo. A defensive cost/phasing signal.

reasons.summary: Scope shrinking vs prior — Amount cut from $200k (Mar) to $175k (Jun) and the buyer explicitly wants a phased, module-by-module rollout given limited change-management appetite; CLM and risk were deprioritized on the 11 Feb dem. Buyer likely getting defensive on cost or implementation/integration (phased over big-bang) — watch.
reasons.detail: Amount cut from $200k (Mar) to $175k (Jun) and the buyer explicitly wants a phased, module-by-module rollout given limited change-management appetite; CLM and risk were deprioritized on the 11 Feb demo. A defensive cost/phasing signal.
reasons.from: ~$200k full-suite S2P intended across all regions
reasons.to: $175k phased rollout (~200 e-proc/Merlin users) starting with priority modules

reasons.detail: On 22 June the deal moved to Shortlisted (top 3) but the close date was pushed from 28 Aug to 1 Oct 2026 (+34 days) because Arabian Industries' exec team paused all non-essential projects amid the war, with restart only 'expected' July/August and a decision now deferred to Q3. This is a Best Case forecasted $175k deal whose economic buyer (CFO Mahesh Nair) has never been engaged and whose sole champion Tushar Patel is only mid-level, so an open-ended war-pause could quietly harden into indefinite deprioritization - or let GEP/Ariba/iValua secure CFO engagement first while we sit idle.
reasons.metric: Close date pushed 34 days (Aug 28 -> Oct 1); restart deferred to Jul/Aug, decision to Q3
reasons.ceo_ask: Ask Mohamad whether Arabian Industries has actually committed to a July/August restart or if the war-pause is open-ended, and what the concrete plan is to get CFO Mahesh Nair engaged before a competitor does.
reasons.summary: Forecasted $175k Arabian Industries slips 34 days as buyer pauses all non-essential projects
reasons.evidence: CloseDate changed 2026-08-28 -> 2026-10-01 on 2026-06-22; Next_Step__c: 'Confirmed we are shortlisted (top 3), but due to the war, all non-essential projects are paused by exec team, expecting to restart in July/August ... decision likely in Q3.'

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is one of three shortlisted vendors for Arabian Industries' greenfield S2P digitization with Tushar Patel driving it, but the win hinges on surviving a war-imposed project freeze and finally reaching the CFO who owns an as-yet-unassigned budget.

win_position.text: Scores in the mid-50s: Zycus cleared the RFP into the final three (Next Step, 22 Jun) with strong functional fit shown on the 11 Feb demo, but Shortlisted caps confidence and the read is held down by an unengaged economic buyer and a frozen project.
The economic-buyer risk is real: Tushar told us on 11 Dec the CFO decides with the MD and budget is not yet assigned, and five months later neither executive has been met.
Scope has drifted defensively from ~$200k to $175k with a deliberately phased rollout, signalling the buyer is managing cost and change appetite, which trims the win read.

deal_momentum.text: The buyer reconfirmed the top-3 shortlist on 22 Jun and answered Dan Quinn's emails through mid-June, so the relationship is live.
Momentum is externally stalled: Arabian's exec team froze all non-essential projects for the regional war, with restart only expected July/August (Next Step, 22 Jun).
No buyer call has been recorded since the 11 Feb demo (nearly five months), so recent progress is email-only, not deepening engagement.

customer_commitment.text: Tushar invested in a full RFP, several clarification rounds and reconfirmed Zycus in the top three, showing genuine intent (activities Mar-Jun 2026).
Commitment is capped by no assigned budget and no executive sponsor; CFO and MD sign-off has not even started.

deal_risk.text: The 1 Oct close date is not credible: a July/August restart plus deeper demos, commercials and CFO approval cannot finish in time, so Best Case is indefensible (recommend Pipeline).
Single-threading on a mid-level specialist is the structural risk; if the CFO deprioritizes S2P after restart, the deal stalls with no exec relationship to save it.

deal_momentum.evidence: Exec-imposed project freeze; no buyer call since 11 Feb 2026

executive_alignment.evidence: CFO/MD never engaged; single-threaded on Tushar Patel

deal_risk.evidence: Oct close not credible; budget unassigned; scope reduced

critical_signals.text: Zycus is confirmed one of the final three after the RFP, with no rival named as preferred and no incumbent S2P suite to unseat, only manual processes.
The 22 June update confirmed the top-three shortlist but Arabian's executives have frozen all non-essential projects for the regional conflict, with a restart only expected in July or August.
The CFO who owns the budget and the MD who co-decides have never been met, leaving the deal single-threaded on Tushar Patel five months after he offered a CFO introduction.
Budget is still unassigned and the deal has drifted from about $200k to $175k on a phased scope, so the CFO must be sold on the value before any spend is approved.

customer_preference.evidence: On the 11 Feb demo Tushar said 'whatever we are looking for, most of the features are already there' and pulled Zycus-specific next steps (spend-analysis session, blanket-PO demo, CFO briefing), but expressed no preference over any competitor.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only - transparency, efficiency, control across a manual multi-region operation; no ROI/savings quantified and Zycus's business-case service twice deferred to the CFO.

items.action: Have new owner Mohamad Alhakim send Tushar a personal note confirming the restart and offering a CFO briefing.

items.expected_effect: Re-establishes a senior Zycus relationship and reserves the exec slot before the restart window opens.

items.action: Convert Tushar's offered CFO introduction into a booked high-level Zycus value session.
items.trigger: Tushar committed on 11 Feb to 'arrange one call along with CFO' but has not delivered it in five months
items.expected_effect: Engages the economic buyer who owns the unassigned budget and de-risks single-threading.

items.action: Build a quantified ROI model tied to Arabian's manual-process pain to anchor the $175k.
items.trigger: Budget is unassigned and only qualitative pain (transparency, efficiency) has been articulated
items.expected_effect: Gives the CFO a value case to approve budget and defends price against phased scope pressure.

items.action: Run a competitive review positioning Merlin agentic negotiation against GEP and Ariba.
items.trigger: Shortlisted alongside GEP/Ariba/Ivalua with no competitive review logged in the last 30 days
items.expected_effect: Sharpens the differentiation carried into the deeper demos while no rival is yet preferred.

items.action: Pre-plan the deeper iAnalyze spend-analysis and blanket-PO demo for the restart.
items.trigger: Tushar requested a spend-analysis session and a blanket-PO/call-off walkthrough on 11 Feb
items.expected_effect: Advances technical validation on the exposed rate-card requirement toward vendor selection.

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — Amount cut from $200k (Mar) to $175k (Jun) and the buyer explicitly wants a phased, module-by-module rollout given limited change-management appetite; CLM and r (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)
Verdict flags close-date risk this sweep.
Next Step carries 2 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.
Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.win_position: Win 53 is carried by preference strength +0.40 (weight 20); champion strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — Amount cut from $200k (Mar) to $175k (Jun) and the buyer explicitly wants a phased, module-by-module rollout….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 59 (moving forward): commercial topics entering (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 21 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 35 is anchored on the stage win (53), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 59, and dragged by risk 21. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.header: Arabian Industries LLC — Shortlisted · $175K · closes 1 Oct
cro_panel.intro: Zycus is one of three shortlisted vendors for Arabian Industries' greenfield S2P digitization with Tushar Patel driving it, but the win hinges on surviving a war-imposed project freeze and finally reaching the CFO who owns an as-yet-unassigned budget.

blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: Scores in the mid-50s: Zycus cleared the RFP into the final three (Next Step, 22 Jun) with strong functional fit shown on the 11 Feb demo, but Shortlisted caps confidence and the read is held down by an unengaged economic buyer and a frozen project.
The economic-buyer risk is real: Tushar told us on 11 Dec the CFO decides with the MD and budget is not yet assigned, and five months later neither executive has been met.
Scope has drifted defensively from ~$200k to $175k with a deliberately phased rollout, signalling the buyer is managing cost and change appetite, which trims the win read.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Win signals and momentum lift the read to 53.

blocks.title: Deal momentum
blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.

bullets.text: The buyer reconfirmed the top-3 shortlist on 22 Jun and answered Dan Quinn's emails through mid-June, so the relationship is live.
Momentum is externally stalled: Arabian's exec team froze all non-essential projects for the regional war, with restart only expected July/August (Next Step, 22 Jun).
No buyer call has been recorded since the 11 Feb demo (nearly five months), so recent progress is email-only, not deepening engagement.

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.

bullets.text: The 1 Oct close date is not credible: a July/August restart plus deeper demos, commercials and CFO approval cannot finish in time, so Best Case is indefensible (recommend Pipeline).
Single-threading on a mid-level specialist is the structural risk; if the CFO deprioritizes S2P after restart, the deal stalls with no exec relationship to save it.

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Zycus is confirmed shortlisted (top 3) as of 22 Jun 2026 with no rival stated as preferred and no S2P incumbent to displace (greenfield replacing manual processes + Epicor).

blocks.title: Customer commitment
blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

bullets.text: Tushar invested in a full RFP, several clarification rounds and reconfirmed Zycus in the top three, showing genuine intent (activities Mar-Jun 2026).
Commitment is capped by no assigned budget and no executive sponsor; CFO and MD sign-off has not even started.

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Have new owner Mohamad Alhakim send Tushar a personal note confirming the restart and offering a CFO briefing. (by 13 Jul)
Convert Tushar's offered CFO introduction into a booked high-level Zycus value session. (by 20 Jul)
Build a quantified ROI model tied to Arabian's manual-process pain to anchor the $175k. (by 31 Jul)

gaps: No transcribed buyer call since 11 Feb 2026; recent engagement is email/Next Step only.
Economic buyer (CFO) and decision maker (MD) known by role but not named in any Salesforce contact role and never engaged.
No assigned budget and no quantified business case.
